# Self-Contained Checklist

Use this checklist before considering `PLAN.md` complete.

- [ ] The feature or change is described as a user or system outcome, not just a technical task.
- [ ] The opening explains why the work matters and how a human can see it working.
- [ ] The owning domain, project, or host is identified.
- [ ] The plan references real repo locations, projects, or patterns where they are known.
- [ ] `Context and Orientation` defines non-obvious terms in plain language and assumes no prior repo knowledge.
- [ ] Ordered work stages or milestones are concrete, implementation-oriented, and independently verifiable when milestones are used.
- [ ] `Concrete Steps` contains exact commands, working directories, and expected outputs or observations.
- [ ] `Validation and Acceptance` includes build, test, and behavior checks scoped to the affected area.
- [ ] `Idempotence and Recovery` explains safe reruns, cleanup, and fallback or retry paths.
- [ ] `Artifacts and Notes` includes concise evidence snippets when evidence is important to prove success.
- [ ] `Interfaces and Dependencies` names the expected interfaces, types, libraries, or services to exist at the end.
- [ ] Assumptions and unresolved decisions are explicit.
- [ ] Risks, compatibility concerns, or migration concerns are called out when relevant.
- [ ] `Progress` is initialized as a timestamped checklist for later execution updates.
- [ ] `Decision Log`, `Surprises & Discoveries`, `Outcomes & Retrospective`, and `Revision Note` are present and ready to be updated during execution.
- [ ] Narrative sections are prose-first rather than template bullets or tables.
- [ ] A novice engineer could use the document to implement the change without a separate planning conversation.
